authority_boundary.fixed_between_variants: 9000-tree target, 6.5 m spacing, 24 m river setback, project-owned eight-form coigue meshes, V12 runtime-far representation, V22 RGB-padded leaf atlas, alpha2 no-shadow material, source masks, terrain, water, rocks, cameras, lighting, physics, and saved map
authority_boundary.changed_in_area_sampled_variant: direct deterministic two-dimensional candidates in a 2.2 km camera-local square, true nearest-centerline distance, probabilistic source-mask feathering, and broad plus mid-scale stand-density fields

accepted_findings: Both area-sampled views reach 9000 trees with deterministic placement, source vegetation and water masks, slope and elevation rejection, transient collision-free ownership, and no saved-map or physics changes.

Direct two-dimensional candidates and nearest-centerline distance remove the V24 dependence on centerline progress plus lateral offsets; the strongest contour-following rows and parallel trunk fences are visibly reduced.
Broad and mid-scale stand fields produce more irregular local boundaries and materially change 10.58 percent of guide-seat pixels and 8.28 percent of river-eye pixels.
The measured 98.03 m and 120.23 m minimum accepted river distances exceed the authored 24 m exclusion because the source vegetation mask and terrain rules provide additional clearance in these views.
rejection_reasons: The mostly binary source vegetation mask still resolves into broad hard forest bands; scalar luma feathering does not create a spatially meaningful transition where the mask lacks a graded edge.

The direct square sampler moves more instances into the runtime-far band and leaves only 813 and 1132 near-plus-mid trees in the two views, so density allocation is not yet a representative moving-camera workload.
A single repeated coigue family cannot provide age, crown, species, riparian, understory, epiphyte, snag, or deadwood diversity and still exposes uniform trunk spacing at stand edges.

Smooth 30 m terrain, tan provisional ground, flat tiled water, sparse boulder and gravel structure, clear atmosphere, and absent mist or spray remain more visually dominant than the topology improvement.
No moving-camera, wind, masked-overdraw, streaming, packaged desktop, console, handheld, or on-device VR evidence exists for this 9000-tree local profile.
next_iteration_requirements: Derive a deterministic spatial vegetation occupancy or signed-distance field from the source mask, then use it for true edge feathering, irregular treeline transitions, stand holes, and river-distance gradients.

Allocate density by camera-independent world cells or streamed reach tiles so guide-seat and river-eye views share one stable forest and the near, mid, and runtime-far workload reflects a moving boat.
Add ecology-reviewed age and vertical strata: coigue adults and intermediates, a second accepted native canopy family, riparian shrubs, bamboo or fern understory, epiphyte cues, snags, and deadwood.

Pair the next ecology pass with world-aligned terrain detail, source-consistent forest-floor color, improved turquoise water response, boulder and gravel-bar structure, humid mountain atmosphere, valley cloud, and spray.
Profile moving-camera temporal stability, wind, HISM culling, masked overdraw, memory, streaming, and packaged desktop, console, handheld, and VR cost before production promotion.
